key_insight: A single unified analytical model can both diagnose where photoelectrochemical cells lose energy and translate those losses into concrete design improvements across different device architectures.
engineering_relevance: It gives engineers a quantitative way to compare materials and structures, fit experimental I–V curves, and prioritize optimizations such as better interfaces, co-catalysts, and nanostructuring.
method: Unifies built-in junction and semiconductor-electrolyte junction PEC models under one physically meaningful parameter set.
Generates ideal and real current–voltage curves, efficiency maps, and Sankey energy-flow diagrams.
Fits experimental I–V data to decompose losses into thermodynamic, optical, recombination, and interfacial terms.
evidence_signal: The framework was validated against literature results across solar water splitting, CO2 reduction, NH3 synthesis, and solar redox flow batteries, including comparisons for Si, perovskites, hematite, and BiVO4.
watch_item: Because the model relies on fitting and parameter interpretation, its recommendations depend on the quality and completeness of experimental I–V data and the validity of the assumed physical losses.
paper_title: From Loss Diagnosis to Rational Design: A Unified Analytical Model for Photoelectrochemical Cells
